title: How to Stop Being Boring (And Why It Matters)
subtitle: 告别乏味：成为有趣之人的秘诀

paragraphs.en: Humans are creatures of habit. We love to establish a routine and stick with it, then often put ourselves on auto-pilot.
paragraphs.zh: 人类是习惯性动物。我们喜欢建立一套日常规律并坚持下去，然后便常常开启自动驾驶模式。

words.meaning: phr. 习惯的奴隶，习性固定的动物

paragraphs.en: Routines can be incredibly useful in helping you get things done. However, too much of a routine can also make you incredibly boring. Nevertheless, many people live lives that are boringly predictable, or live a life where everything is outlined or planned.
paragraphs.zh: 日常规律在帮你完成任务方面极为有用。但过度的规律也会让你变得极其乏味。尽管如此，许多人仍过着无聊到可预见的生活，或者一切都被规划得井井有条。

words.meaning: adj. 可预见的，墨守成规的

paragraphs.en: To tell the truth, interesting people are more popular among their friends. If you don't arouse someone's curiosity or brighten someone's day, you probably come across as being a little bit dull. But that doesn't mean your life has ended and you can't do anything to change it.
paragraphs.zh: 说实话，有趣的人在朋友圈中更受欢迎。如果你无法激起他人的好奇心或点亮他们的一天，恐怕就会给人留下些许沉闷的印象。但这并不意味着你的人生就此完蛋、无法改变。

paragraphs.en: If you find yourself searching for something to say beyond small talk, try these tactics to find more interesting approaches to conversation. Recently, I was at a gathering of colleagues when someone turned to me and asked, "So, what's new with you?"
paragraphs.zh: 如果你发现自己除了寒暄便无话可说，不妨试试以下策略，寻找更有趣的交谈方式。最近，在一次同事聚会上，有人转向我问道：“你最近有什么新鲜事吗？”

paragraphs.en: Ordinarily, I think I'm a good conversationalist. After all, it's literally my job to talk to people and tell their stories or share their advice. And that's not exactly an unexpected question.
paragraphs.zh: 平时我自认为是个健谈的人。毕竟，跟人聊天、讲述他们的故事或分享建议，确实是我的本职工作。而且这个问题也不算突兀。

words.meaning: n. 健谈的人，善于辞令者
adv. 确实地，毫不夸张地

paragraphs.en: Still, the only "new-to-me" topics that came to mind were my daughter's basketball tournament and my feelings about that morning's political headlines — neither amusing nor appropriate topics at that moment. Oh, no, I thought. Have I become boring?
paragraphs.zh: 尽管如此，我脑中蹦出的“新鲜”话题只有女儿的篮球联赛以及我对当日政治头条的看法——这两者在当时既无趣又不合时宜。糟了，我想，难道我已经变得无聊了吗？

paragraphs.en: But sharing our experiences in an authentic way to connect with other people is what makes us interesting, says associate professor Michael Pirson. The hesitation I felt in not sharing the ordinary things that were happening in my life, and the wild mental search for something more interesting, may have backfired and made me seem less interesting.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，副教授Michael Pirson表示，以真实的方式分享自身经历并与他人建立联结，正是我们显得有趣的原因。我不愿分享生活中的平凡琐事而产生的犹豫，以及疯狂搜寻更有趣话题的心理活动，反而可能弄巧成拙，让我显得更无趣。

words.meaning: v. 事与愿违，适得其反

paragraphs.en: "If someone is making up some conversation that might be interesting, it's probably not going to land well," says Pirson, whose expertise includes trust and well-being, mindfulness, and humanistic management. "It's going to feel like a made-up conversation that people don't necessarily want to tune in to."
paragraphs.zh: Pirson专攻信任与幸福感、正念以及人本管理领域，他说：“如果一个人编造一些看似有趣的对话，效果多半不会好。那感觉就像一段虚构的对话，人们未必想听下去。”

paragraphs.en: The most interesting people aren't those who've gone on some Eat, Pray, Love journey to find themselves. Instead, Pirson says, they're those who examine the ordinary. "Often, the 'boring things' may not be boring at all. Maybe they are actually little miracles," he says.
paragraphs.zh: 最有趣的人并非那些踏上《美食、祈祷、恋爱》式旅程去寻找自我的人。相反，Pirson说，他们是那些审视平凡的人。他说：“那些‘无聊的事’往往一点也不无聊，说不定它们正是小小的奇迹。”

paragraphs.en: Share your observations about the world around you — interesting stories you heard or things you noticed — and you may be surprised by the universal connection they inspire. This is essentially how Jessica Hagy starts her day.
paragraphs.zh: 分享你对周遭世界的观察——你听到的趣闻或留意到的事物——你或许会惊讶地发现它们能引发普遍的共鸣。这基本上就是Jessica Hagy开启一天的方式。

paragraphs.en: The author of How to Be Interesting: An Instruction Manual, Hagy spends a lot of time thinking about what's interesting to her. People who are interesting are persistently curious, she says.
paragraphs.zh: 作为《如何变得有趣：一本指导手册》的作者，Hagy花了大量时间思考什么对她而言是有趣的。她说，有趣的人是那些保有持续好奇心的人。

words.meaning: adv. 持续地，坚持不懈地

paragraphs.en: Think about the everyday things around you and ask questions about them. What is that roadside monument I see on my way to work every day? Who built that interesting building in my city?
paragraphs.zh: 想一想你身边的日常事物，并对它们提出问题。我每天上班路上看到的那座路边纪念碑是什么来头？我所在城市里那座有趣的建筑是谁建的？

paragraphs.en: What nearby attractions haven't I visited? Why do people do things that way? Use what you find to ask more questions and learn more about the world around you.
paragraphs.zh: 附近还有哪些景点我没去过？为什么人们要以那种方式行事？利用你的发现去追问更多问题，从而更深入地了解你周遭的世界。

paragraphs.en: "Having that sort of curiosity is almost like a protective gear from getting into boredom," she says. And when you find things that are truly interesting to you, share them.
paragraphs.zh: 她说：“拥有那种好奇心几乎就像是防止陷入无聊的护具。”而且，当你发现了真正令自己着迷的东西，就去分享吧。

paragraphs.en: Television veteran Audrey Morrissey, executive producer of NBC's The Voice, is always looking for what will make a person or story interesting to viewers. It's usually a matter of individuality. "Having a strong point of view, signature style, or being a super-enthusiast in a particular field makes someone interesting," she says.
paragraphs.zh: 电视行业资深人士、NBC《美国好声音》的执行制片人Audrey Morrissey总是在寻找能让一个人或一个故事吸引观众的元素。这通常关乎个性。她说：“拥有鲜明的观点、标志性的风格，或者在某个领域极度狂热，都会让一个人变得有趣。”

words.meaning: n. 经验丰富的人，老手

paragraphs.en: That means embracing what is truly interesting or unique about yourself. "Many people are 'not boring' in the way that they can carry a conversation or can be good at a social gathering. To be interesting means that you have lived life, taken risks, traveled, sought out experience to learn for yourself and share with others," she says.
paragraphs.zh: 这意味着要接纳自己身上真正有趣或独特的地方。她说：“很多人所谓的‘不无聊’仅仅体现在能接话茬或擅长社交应酬。真正的有趣意味着你真实地活过、冒过险、旅行过，主动寻找过经历来为自己学习并与他人分享。”

paragraphs.en: Of course, it's possible to be a fountain of knowledge and a boring person, says public relations consultant Andrea Pass. Paying attention to the listener is an important part of having a conversation that's interesting to both parties. Talking on and on about what's interesting to you isn't going to make you an interesting person, she says.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，一个人完全可能既是知识源泉又是无聊之人，公关顾问Andrea Pass说。关注倾听者是让对话对双方都有趣的重要环节。她表示，滔滔不绝地谈论自己感兴趣的事并不会让你成为一个有趣的人。

paragraphs.en: "If the listener is not paying attention, it's your sign to shorten the story or change direction. Make sure to bring the audience into the conversation so that it is not one-sided," Pass says. Be a better listener yourself, and give others opportunities to participate in the conversation by inviting them with questions or requests to share their own experiences or thoughts.
paragraphs.zh: Pass说：“如果听者走神，那就是你该缩短故事或转换话题的信号。务必让听众参与进来，避免自说自话。”你自己也要做一个更好的倾听者，并通过提问或邀请对方分享自身经历和想法，给予他人参与对话的机会。

words.meaning: adj. 单方面的，一边倒的

paragraphs.en: Questions are a powerful tool, especially when they encourage others to disclose information about themselves. A 2012 study from the University of California, Santa Barbara, found that roughly 40% of the time we are talking, we're disclosing subjective information about our experience.
paragraphs.zh: 提问是一个强有力的工具，尤其当问题能鼓励他人透露自身信息时更是如此。加州大学圣巴巴拉分校2012年的一项研究发现，我们在说话时大约有40%的时间都在分享有关自身经历的主观信息。

paragraphs.en: And when we're doing so, our brains are more engaged. So one strategy to leave others with the impression that you're a sparkling conversation partner is to get others to talk about themselves. Being relatable is also essential, Morrissey says.
paragraphs.zh: 而当我们这样做时，大脑的参与度会更高。因此，要给他人留下你是个出色交谈者的印象，策略之一便是引导对方谈论他们自己。Morrissey说，能让人产生共鸣也至关重要。

words.meaning: adj. 妙趣横生的，出色的
adj. 能引起共鸣的，可理解的

paragraphs.en: "The best entertainment and storytelling comes from people who are relatable — those who don't shy away from opening up but freely share who they are and what they care about. These are the people viewers most relate to and find interesting. Being authentic, honest, and vulnerable is always interesting."
paragraphs.zh: “最好的娱乐和故事讲述都源自那些能引发共鸣的人——他们不回避敞开心扉，而是坦然分享自己是谁以及在乎什么。这些才是观众最能认同且觉得有趣的人。真实、坦诚且愿意示弱，永远都充满趣味。”

paragraphs.en: I have now come to realize that being boring, in actuality, is not only about who you are as a person, but also how you present yourself. No matter what, make sure you are having fun in life. Because when you are enjoying, people around you will begin to enjoy as well.
paragraphs.zh: 如今我意识到，所谓无聊，其实不仅关乎你是什么样的人，也关乎你如何展现自己。无论如何，务必让自己在生活中找到乐趣。因为当你乐在其中时，身边的人也会随之被感染。

paragraphs.en: Show some interest in them and they will definitely show some in you. If you are a very reserved person, this could be a little difficult at first. But with a little effort, you can definitely improve.
paragraphs.zh: 对他们表现出一些兴趣，他们必定也会投桃报李。如果你是个很内向的人，起初可能有点难。但只要稍加努力，你一定可以有所改进。
